name: Vite
what_it_does: Next-generation frontend build tool that uses native ES modules for a fast dev server with HMR, and Rollup for optimized production builds.
use_cases: Scaffold and run a React, Vue, or Svelte project with instant HMR using 'npm create vite@latest' and 'vite dev'
Produce an optimized production bundle for a single-page application via 'vite build', which runs Rollup under the hood
Use the JavaScript API (createServer, build, preview) to programmatically drive Vite inside a custom agent build pipeline
Apply Rollup-compatible plugins (e.g., @rollup/plugin-node-resolve) through Vite's unified plugin interface
Configure SSR builds with vite.config.ts 'ssr' option for server-rendered frameworks like SvelteKit or Remix
not_for: Non-JavaScript build pipelines — Vite is tightly coupled to the JS/TS ecosystem and is not a general-purpose task runner
Legacy browser targets requiring CommonJS output without ESM — Vite's dev server is ESM-native and older environments need extra config
Bare Node.js library bundling — Rollup directly (without Vite) is simpler and lighter for pure library output
best_when: Building a modern frontend SPA or SSR app where dev-server startup time and HMR speed are critical, and you want a unified config for dev and production.
avoid_when: You need to target environments without ES module support or you are bundling a Node.js library rather than a frontend application.

auth.notes: Local build tool — no authentication needed.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Vite provides rich, color-coded terminal error output with file and line references; HMR errors surface clearly in the browser console.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Production builds via 'vite build' are deterministic for the same source and config.
known_agent_gotchas: Vite 5+ requires Node.js 18 or later — agents must verify the Node version before scaffolding or running a Vite project
Dev server uses native ESM; importing CommonJS-only packages can cause 'does not provide an export named' errors that require explicit 'optimizeDeps' configuration
SSR builds require a separate 'ssr' entry config; the default SPA build config will not produce a correct SSR bundle
Environment variables must be prefixed with VITE_ to be exposed to client code — other .env vars are stripped silently
Rollup is used for production builds but esbuild is used for dev transpilation; behavior differences (e.g., in decorator handling) can cause dev/prod parity issues

security_score_components.security_notes: Agents must ensure VITE_-prefixed env vars do not expose secrets to the browser bundle. Dependency count is higher than esbuild; pin versions in CI.
